name: Richard H. Kirk
description:
'''Richard Harold Kirk''' (21 March 1956 – 21 September 2021) was an English composer, musician and producer. He is best known for his work in [[electronic music]], and for co-founding the influential music group [[Cabaret Voltaire (band)|Cabaret Voltaire]] in 1973 with [[Chris Watson (musician)|Chris Watson]] and [[Stephen Mallinder]]. 

Outside of Cabaret Voltaire, he was part of bands such as [[Sweet Exorcist (band)|Sweet Exorcist]] and [[Acid Horse]], and collaborated with bands and artists such as Peter Hope and [[Kora (band)|Kora]].

As a solo artist, Kirk released music under his own name as well as under dozens of [[Pseudonym|aliases]]. His prolific solo work explored various genres, including [[Dub music|dub]], [[techno]], [[Ambient music|ambient]], [[Electro (music)|electro]], [[Industrial music|industrial]] and [[acid house]].
